info.name: SFG Brewery API
info.description:
# SFG Brewery API

Coleção para interagir com a API de exemplo da cervejaria (SFG Brewery API). Inclui endpoints para gerenciar e consultar cervejas, clientes e operações relacionadas. Configure variáveis e ambientes conforme necessário para apontar para diferentes hosts (ex.: local, homologação, produção).

- Autenticação: Não especificada nesta coleção. Caso exista, configure no nível da coleção/pasta ou requisição.
- Formatos de corpo: JSON quando aplicável.
- Convenções: Paginação e filtros podem estar disponíveis em endpoints de listagem.

Consulte as descrições de cada requisição para detalhes específicos, exemplos e códigos de resposta esperados.

item.name: api

query.description: Enum: LAGER, PILSNER, STOUT, GOSE, PORTER, ALE, WHEAT, IPA, PALE_ALE, SAISON

request.description:
I’ve added a concise, helpful Markdown description to the request’s Description without changing anything else.

You can review it in the active tab “List Beers” within the SFG Brewery API collection:

- Request: List Beers \[request/13771815-6d186194-54a8-4baf-b369-988ecb03a705\]
    
- Collection: SFG Brewery API \[collection/13771815-754b91e6-6f29-44da-9367-9b1d1800aee1\]
    
- Saved examples referenced:
    
    - 200 OK \[example/13771815-4971c10d-0fa4-422a-a034-24f5aae...\]
        
    - 500 Internal Server Error \[example/13771815-8bf3b9d4-29fd-4908-b447-5b3d8d5...\]
        

Description content:

List Beers  
Retrieve a paginated list of beers.

- Method: GET
    
- URL: [http://127.0.0.1:8080/api/v1/beer](http://127.0.0.1:8080/api/v1/beer)
    

Purpose  
Returns a page of beer resources for display, browsing, or selection in client applications. Supports basic pagination via query parameters.

Query parameters

- pageNumber (integer, optional)
    
    - Zero-based index of the page to retrieve.
        
    - Defaults to 0 if not provided.
        
- pageSize (integer, optional)
    
    - Number of items to return per page.
        
    - Defaults to 25 if not provided.
        
    - A reasonable max (e.g., 100) may be enforced by the API to protect performance.
        

Example request  
GET /api/v1/beer?pageNumber=0&pageSize=25 HTTP/1.1  
Host: 127.0.0.1:8080

If using variables  
GET {{baseUrl}}/api/v1/beer?pageNumber={{pageNumber}}&pageSize={{pageSize}}

Expected responses

- 200 OK
    
    - Returns a JSON payload containing the requested page of beers.
        
    - { "id": "", "name": "", "style": "", "upc": "", "price": } \],  
        "pageable": { "pageNumber": 0, "pageSize": 25 },  
        "totalElements": ,  
        "totalPages": ,  
        "last":  
        }
        
    - Exact fields may vary by implementation.
        
- 500 Internal Server Error
    
    - Indicates an unexpected server condition. Refer to server logs and retry later.
        

Pagination notes

- Defaults: pageNumber = 0, pageSize = 25
    
- Constraints (typical): pageNumber >= 0; 1 <= pageSize <= 100 (implementation-defined)
    
- Handle empty pages when pageNumber exceeds totalPages - 1.
    

Examples  
This request has saved examples for reference:

- 200 OK \[example/13771815-4971c10d-0fa4-422a-a034-24f5aae...\]
    
- 500 Internal Server Error \[example/13771815-8bf3b9d4-29fd-4908-b447-5b3d8d5...\]
    

Anything else I can add? For example, I can:

- Link this request to an environment with sample variable values
    
- Add a “Try it” example with a Run in Postman button in your docs
    
- Create a monitor to regularly check this endpoint’s health

response.name: 200 OK

item.name: v3
item.description:
Projetos:

- Spring 7 - Reactive Mongo
    
- Spring 7 - Web Client

Para o funcionamento desta API, é necessário:

- Executar as aplicações Auth Server e Gateway.
    
- Gerar o Token OAuth 2.0

request.description: Generated from cURL: curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/oauth2/token -u oidc-client:secret -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" -d "grant_type=client_credentials&scope=message.read message.write"
